# Skill Writing Guidelines

<!-- Generated by skill-conductor setup. Customize for your project. -->

## SKILL.md Format Rules

### CRITICAL: Description Field
- **MUST be a single-line string** (use double quotes `"..."`)
- **NEVER use YAML multi-line format** (`|` or `>`) — causes silent discovery failure
- **MUST be ≤ 1024 characters** — longer descriptions get truncated
- Structure: `[What it does] + [When to use it] + [Trigger keywords]`

```yaml
# ✅ CORRECT
description: "What the skill does. Use when: specific trigger scenarios. Triggers: keyword1, keyword2, 关键词1."

# ❌ WRONG — will not be discovered
description: |
  What the skill does.
  Use when: specific trigger scenarios.
```

### Name Field
- Lowercase letters, numbers, and hyphens only
- Max 64 characters
- Should match directory name

### SKILL.md Length
- Keep under 500 lines
- Move detailed reference material to separate files

## Trigger Word Best Practices
- Include both literal and natural language variations
- Cover formal and casual phrasings
- Include 中英文 if bilingual project
- Avoid overly generic words that would trigger on unrelated queries
- List specific domain terms

## Safety Boundary Pattern
Every skill should include a safety section:
```markdown
## Safety Boundaries
- DO NOT: diagnose, prescribe, or replace professional advice
- DO: provide information, suggest next steps, recommend consulting experts
```

## Output Format Template
Every skill should include a consistent output format:
```markdown
## Output Format
Always use this template:
# [Title]
## [Section 1]
## [Section 2]
```

## Cross-Reference Pattern
When skills reference each other, use this pattern:
```markdown
## Cross-References
| This skill | Related skill | Relationship |
|------------|---------------|-------------|
| skill-a | skill-b | data flow / trigger / complement |
```
